data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: M, ADBE and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Wednesday rose, after key consumer inflation data came in-line with estimates. Here are some stocks to watch on Wednesday:

  • Shares of Macy's (M) slumped 11% in pre-market trading, after the department store chain delivered a Q3 drop in comparable sales and cut its annual adjusted profit per share guidance. Weakness in Macy's (M) non-First 50 locations as well as its digital channel and cold weather categories impacted overall quarterly net sales. The company also completed an independent investigation that uncovered $151M of hidden cumulative delivery expenses from Q4 2021 to Q3 2024. See how M performed in charts.
  • Adobe (ADBE) stock will be in focus, ahead of the creative software maker's FQ4 2024 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the San Jose, Calif.-based firm to earn $4.67 per share on revenue of $5.54B. Adobe (ADBE) is known for its Photoshop graphics editor and Premier Pro video editor, but investors recently have increasingly focused on its artificial intelligence (AI) initiatives. ADBE has been continuing the rollout of AI updates to its photography services, and in October introduced over 100 new creative cloud features.
  • Class A shares of Mondelēz (MDLZ) gained nearly 5% ahead of market open, after the confectionary and snacks manufacturer authorized a new stock buyback of up to $9B. Mondelēz (MDLZ) - the owner of popular brands like Cadbury, Oreo and Toblerone - said the new stock repurchase would be effective January 1, 2025. "Given current market conditions, share repurchase remains an opportunity and key priority. The company remains committed to an acquisition strategy that is focused on bolt-on assets similar to recent acquisitions of Chipita, Clif and Ricolino," MDLZ said.
  • General Motors (GM) stock climbed nearly 2% in pre-market trading. The legacy U.S. automaker on Tuesday said it would realign its autonomous driving strategy and prioritize development of advanced driver assistance systems. As part of the plan, GM (GM) will no longer fund its Cruise unit's development of a robotaxi. The announcement by the company has major implications for other robotaxi developers such as Uber (UBER), Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Waymo.

More on the markets

  • SP500 Likely To Underperform In 2025, But Still One Of The Best Long-Term Investments
  • Greenback Bid Ahead Of CPI
  • The 2 Most Important Labor Market Sectors
  • S&P, Dow, Nasdaq end listless session lower with CPI inflation on deck; Oracle slides
  • U.S. small business optimism jumps to highest level in over three years on Trump boost

data.title: Albertson's terminates merger, sues Kroger for breach of agreement
data.description:
After a federal judge ruled in favor of the Federal Trade Commission and agreed to grant a preliminary injunction to block the proposed $25B merger between Albertson’s (NYSE:ACI) and Kroger (NYSE:KR), Albertson’s (ACI) has officially terminated its efforts to merge with the grocery chain and has sued Kroger (KR) for breach of the merger agreement.

Additionally, Albertson’s (ACI) says it is now entitled to the $600M termination fee.

“Given the recent federal and state court decisions to block our proposed merger with Kroger, we have made the difficult decision to terminate the merger agreement. We are deeply disappointed in the courts’ decisions,” Albertson’s CEO Vivek Sankaran said early Wednesday.

Albertson’s (ACI) is pointing the finger at Kroger (KR) for its failure to offer an adequate divestiture package and repeatedly ignoring regulators’ concerns about creating an anti-competitive environment. The lawsuit, filed in the Delaware Court of Chancery, seeks damages of billions of dollars to “make Albertson’s shareholders whole.”

“Rather than fulfill its contractual obligations to ensure that the merger succeeded, Kroger acted in its own financial self-interest, repeatedly providing insufficient divestiture proposals that ignored regulators’ concerns,” Albertson’s general counsel Tom Moriarty said in a statement, confirming that the basis of the lawsuit is supported by rulings from the District Court of Oregon and King County Superior Court in Washington.

The effort to merge the two grocery chains has been plagued by regulatory resistance from the very beginning. The FTC stepped in to block the merger in February, arguing that a combination of the two would be anti-competitive and lead to higher prices for consumers. Eight states and Washington, D.C. joined in the fight to block the deal, prompting Kroger (KR) to make concessions to satisfy regulators, including the sale of 600 of its stores to C&S Wholesalers. However, in her decision, Oregon judge Adrienne Nelson said the divestiture was not sufficient in scale to adequately compete with the merged firm.

Lastly, while the merger would have resulted in a $5.2B windfall for Cerberus Capital Management, the firm said it does not intend to sell its stake in the company and remains confident in its strength as a standalone company.

Shares of Albertson’s (ACI) are climbing in premarket, last trading with a gain of ~3%, while Kroger (KR) shares are dipping into the red.

data.title: SOL Global announces loan repayment
data.description:
• SOL Global Investments (OTCPK:SOLCF) repaid $3.4M of its outstanding debt to its senior lender, Braebeacon Holdings, decreasing the loan balance to $10.2M.

  • This repayment aligns with SOL Global's broader financial objectives and reflects its ongoing commitment to strengthening its financial position and reducing leverage.

  • By strategically paying down this portion of its debt, the company aims to reduce interest expenses and position itself for future growth opportunities.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher acquires Sheila J. Butler & Company
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) said it has acquired Georgia-based Sheila J. Butler & Company.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Sheila J. Butler & Company is an employee benefits brokerage firm.
  • Sheila J. Butler, Blake Butler and their team will remain in their current location, the company said.

data.title: Helix Biopharma appoints James Murphy as CFO
data.description:
• Helix BioPharma (OTCPK:HBPCF) on Wednesday announced the appointment of James Murphy as Chief Financial Officer.
  • Murphy is currently a consultant with Danforth Advisors. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Digital Brands announces 1-for-50 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Digital Brands Group (NASDAQ:DBGI) on Wednesday said it will effectuate a 1-for-50 reverse split of its common stock that will become effective on December 13.
  • The company expects that the reverse stock split will allow it to regain compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement of the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • DBG’s common stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “DBGI” and will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the market opens on December 13.
  • DBGI -21.95% premarket to $0.08.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gouverneur Bancorp approves share buyback program
data.description:
• Gouverneur Bancorp (OTCQB:GOVB) said its board has approved a stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase of up to 55.4K shares.
  • Press Release

data.title: Hepion Pharmaceuticals announces termination of merger agreement with Pharma Two B
data.description:
• Hepion Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:HEPA) on Wednesday said it has entered into a termination agreement with Pharma Two B, which terminates the merger agreement between the two parties that was entered into in July.
  • HEPA -22.23% premarket to $0.4899.
  • Earlier in July, Pharma Two B has entered into a merger agreement with Hepion Pharmaceuticals.
  • Neither party will be required to pay the other a termination fee as a result of the mutual decision to terminate the agreement.
  • The drug developer also announced that its special meeting of its stockholders scheduled for December 12, 2024 has been cancelled.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GreenPower receives follow-on order from Transportation Commodities for 10 EV star cab & chassis
data.description:
• GreenPower Motor (NASDAQ:GP) on Wednesday announced that it has received a follow-on order for 10 EV Star Cab & Chassis from Transportation Commodities.
  • GP +0.06% premarket to $0.9.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CPI read: Prices on sporting goods are in a downtrend ahead of the holidays
data.description:
Prices on sporting goods products in the U.S. fell 2.8% year-over-year in November compared to a year ago on an unadjusted basis. Overall, core inflation was up 3.3% year-over-year during the month, which was level with the pace in September.

On a month-to-month comparison, sporting good prices were down 0.3% in November after falling 0.5% in both October, according to data compiled by the Bureau of Transportation Statistics. Airfares have now decreased in four of the last five months.

The sporting goods category in the Consumer Price Index report encompasses a wide range of equipment and apparel related to sports and recreational activities.

The lower level of prices may not be an indication that demand has fallen off dramatically, based on recent commentary from Dick's Sporting Goods (NYSE:DKS) management.

Sports-related stocks: Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), Academy Sports and Outdoors (NASDAQ:ASO), Hibbett, Big 5 Sporting Goods (BGFV) ,Amer Sports (AS), Topgolf Callaway Brands (MODG), Nike (NYSE:NKE), Under Armour (UAA),
